description: EditorPanel.handleSnapshot mutates the local `blocks` array based on the inbound snapshot in ways that are not specified. Specifically: when an editing snapshot arrives whose `focusedBlockId` is NOT in the current `blocks` array, the handler synthesises a new block `{ id: focusedId, type: 'paragraph', content: '' }` from the focusedBlockId — fabricating block data that the domain never delivered. The block list and block content are owned by the Rust domain (per behavioral-spec §2 'Out of scope: Markdown ↔ Block parsing/serialisation'); the EditingSessionStateDto (verification-architecture §10) carries only status / currentNoteId / focusedBlockId / pendingNextFocus / isNoteEmpty / lastSaveError / lastSaveResult — it does NOT carry the block tree. The shell is silently inventing UI state. Real production behavior depends on the snapshot delivering blocks via a separate channel that this implementation never wires; tests pass because they pre-seed `initialBlocks` and `_emitState` snapshots whose focusedBlockId matches a seeded block.
suggestedRemediation: Either (a) extend EditingSessionStateDto / verification-architecture §10 to carry a `blocks: ReadonlyArray<{id, type, content}>` field on every non-idle arm and remove the synthesis path, or (b) consume a separate `subscribeToBlockTree` inbound channel for block content and treat block-tree updates as a distinct concern from session-state. Either way, the shell must not fabricate blocks from focusedBlockId.
